summary.text: The dominant pattern today is sustained interest in AI agent frameworks and local LLM inference tools. 'pm-skills' (13.3k stars, +897 daily) continues its hot streak as a structured agent skill marketplace, while 'whichllm' (3.9k stars, +740 daily) gains traction for its hardware-aware local LLM benchmarking. The sudden breakout of 'AiToEarn' (19.8k stars, +971 daily) suggests growing developer curiosity in AI-powered content monetization, though this may be a launch-day spike. Computer vision tools 'supervision' and 'opencv' remain steady, indicating ongoing infrastructure demand. On Hacker News, Apple's announcement of a new AI architecture built around Google Gemini models (673 votes) and a discussion titled 'AI is slowing down' (623 votes) signal developer skepticism about frontier model progress, which may indirectly boost interest in local and agent-based alternatives.

projects.name: whichllm
projects.description: Find the local LLM that actually runs and performs best on your hardware. Ranked by real, recency-aware benchmarks, not parameter count. One command, run it instantly.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A CLI tool that auto-detects your hardware and ranks local LLMs by real, recency-aware benchmarks — not just parameter count.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Choosing the best local LLM for your specific hardware is tedious and error-prone. whichllm automates hardware detection, VRAM estimation, and benchmark-aware ranking so you get the best model that actually runs on your machine with one command.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 3,800 stars and a 24-hour delta of +630, whichllm is experiencing a sustained surge in attention. The project has a recent release (within 30 days) and active commits within the last 7 days, indicating ongoing development and community engagement. The acceleration ratio is low (1.0), suggesting steady organic growth rather than a sudden spike.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release and continuous commits likely maintain momentum, and the tool addresses a clear pain point for the growing local LLM community.
llmSummary.whyTrending: whichllm solves a real, common problem for anyone running LLMs locally: figuring out which model works best on their hardware. Its one-command simplicity, live benchmark integration, and GPU simulation for purchase planning make it immediately useful. The combination of practical utility and polished documentation drives word-of-mouth and social sharing.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you run local LLMs, install whichllm to instantly find the best model for your hardware. It saves hours of trial and error and provides actionable insights for hardware upgrades.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward tooling that abstracts away the complexity of local LLM deployment. Developers should expect more 'auto-pilot' tools that handle hardware detection, model selection, and optimization, reducing friction for end users and enabling broader adoption of local AI.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI developers, ML engineers, hobbyists running local LLMs, and anyone evaluating hardware for local inference (e.g., planning a GPU purchase).
llmSummary.riskNotes: The tool relies on HuggingFace API and external benchmark sources; if those change or become unavailable, functionality may degrade. The scoring system uses heuristics and confidence levels that may not always reflect real-world performance. Users should verify critical decisions with their own benchmarks.

projects.name: pm-skills
projects.description: PM Skills Marketplace: 100+ agentic skills, commands, and plugins — from discovery to strategy, execution, launch, and growth.

llmSummary.oneLiner: PM Skills Marketplace is an open-source collection of 100+ agentic skills, commands, and plugins for AI assistants, turning generic AI into a structured product management co-pilot.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Generic AI assistants lack domain-specific frameworks for product management. This repo provides ready-to-use PM skills (e.g., discovery, strategy, execution) that encode proven methodologies, enabling AI to guide users through structured workflows instead of just generating text.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has 13,158 stars and gained 798 stars in the last 24 hours, indicating strong and sustained attention. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), meaning growth is steady rather than explosive, but the consistent high velocity suggests deep community interest. This is not a flash-in-the-pan; it's a project with staying power.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release (within 30 days) and active commits (within 7 days) show ongoing maintenance, but the star velocity is likely driven by word-of-mouth and the growing need for structured AI workflows in product management.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It addresses a clear pain point: product managers want AI that understands their domain, not just a chatbot. The marketplace model (skills, commands, plugins) is easy to adopt, and the integration with Claude Code and Cowork lowers the barrier. The MIT license and PR-friendly culture encourage community contributions.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a blueprint for building domain-specific AI skill marketplaces. Developers can learn how to structure skills, commands, and plugins for other verticals (e.g., marketing, engineering, design) and see how to make them compatible with multiple AI assistants.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The project signals a shift from general-purpose AI to specialized, framework-driven agents. For AI developers, this means investing in domain-specific skill repositories could be a high-impact strategy. The plugin architecture is portable across assistants, reducing lock-in risk.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Product managers, AI assistant power users, developers building AI workflows, and anyone using Claude Code, Cowork, Codex CLI, or other AI tools for product development.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Heavy reliance on Claude-specific commands may limit adoption on other platforms. The quality of skills depends on community contributions, which could vary. Also, the project is young and may not have long-term maintenance guarantees.

projects.name: supervision
projects.description: We write your reusable computer vision tools. 💜

llmSummary.oneLiner: A reusable, model-agnostic Python toolkit for computer vision tasks like detection, segmentation, tracking, and dataset management.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers waste time reinventing common CV utilities (annotators, dataset converters, trackers). Supervision provides a unified, low-code library that works with any model (YOLO, Transformers, etc.) to accelerate building CV applications.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 42,785 stars and a 24-hour delta of +833, this project shows sustained high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), indicating steady organic interest rather than a sudden spike. It's a top-tier CV utility library with massive community adoption.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits and ongoing documentation updates keep it fresh, but no major release or external event is evident.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It solves a universal pain point for CV engineers: reducing boilerplate. The library is model-agnostic, well-documented, and backed by Roboflow, giving it credibility and a growing ecosystem.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you build CV applications, Supervision can cut development time significantly. It's worth adopting as a standard dependency for annotation, tracking, and dataset handling.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward modular, reusable CV tooling. Developers no longer need to build custom pipelines from scratch; they can compose solutions from libraries like Supervision, accelerating prototyping and production.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Computer vision engineers, ML practitioners, data scientists, and hobbyists building object detection, segmentation, or tracking applications.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Heavy reliance on Roboflow's ecosystem may introduce vendor lock-in for some features. The library is still evolving, so API stability is not guaranteed.

projects.name: opencv

llmSummary.oneLiner: The de facto open-source library for computer vision and image processing, now integrating deep learning capabilities.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Provides a comprehensive, optimized set of tools for real-time computer vision tasks, from basic image manipulation to advanced deep learning inference, eliminating the need to build vision pipelines from scratch.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 88,427 stars and a sustained 222-star daily delta over the past three days, OpenCV continues to attract massive attention. The acceleration ratio of 1 indicates steady, organic growth rather than a sudden spike. This consistent velocity reflects its essential role in AI and robotics workflows.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits and a release within 30 days show active maintenance, but no specific event explains the current momentum.
llmSummary.whyTrending: OpenCV remains the foundational library for computer vision in AI applications, from autonomous vehicles to medical imaging. Its deep learning module and Python bindings make it accessible to a broad developer audience, driving continuous adoption.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: OpenCV is a must-know library for any developer working on vision-based AI. Its extensive documentation, community, and integration with frameworks like TensorFlow and PyTorch make it a safe, high-ROI investment.
llmSummary.developerInsight: For AI developers, OpenCV signals the maturation of computer vision as a commodity capability. The library's sustained popularity indicates that vision is becoming a standard component in AI systems, not a niche specialty.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Computer vision engineers, robotics developers, AI researchers, and any developer building applications that require image or video analysis.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The large codebase and broad scope can be overwhelming for newcomers. Some advanced features may lag behind cutting-edge research, and performance optimization requires careful use of C++ APIs.

projects.name: AiToEarn

llmSummary.oneLiner: An open-source AI agent platform for content marketing and monetization across 14+ social platforms, targeting solo entrepreneurs and creators.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Creators and small businesses waste time manually publishing, engaging, and monetizing content across multiple platforms. AiToEarn automates the entire workflow with AI agents, from content creation to cross-platform distribution and performance-based monetization.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo gained 873 stars in the last 24 hours (309/day average) with a sudden breakout pattern, reaching 19,663 total stars. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), but the rule-based trend type is 'sudden_breakout', indicating a sharp increase in attention. This is likely driven by the recent v2.4 release (May 21, 2026) adding new AI models and enhanced batch generation, combined with growing interest in AI-powered content automation tools.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The latest release v2.4.0 (May 21, 2026) introduced support for HappyHorse 1.0 and Seedance 2.0 for draft generation, enhanced batch video/image generation, multi-model selection, and a new UI. The README was also updated to highlight these features, which likely triggered the star surge.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It combines two hot trends: AI agents and content creator economy. The project offers a complete, open-source solution for content automation and monetization, with a clear value proposition for solo entrepreneurs and small teams. The recent release and active community (3k forks) amplify its visibility.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This project demonstrates how to build a multi-platform content automation tool using AI agents. Developers can learn from its architecture (MCP protocol support, Docker deployment, Electron client) and consider integrating similar agent-based workflows into their own tools.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The rise of AI-powered content marketing agents signals a shift toward autonomous content operations. Developers should explore MCP protocol integration and agent-based automation for social media management, as this could become a standard workflow for creator tools.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Solo entrepreneurs (OPC), content creators, small businesses, and marketing teams who want to automate content publishing, engagement, and monetization across multiple social platforms.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project relies on third-party platform APIs (Douyin, Xiaohongshu, etc.) which may change or restrict access. The monetization model (CPS/CPE/CPM) depends on advertiser demand and platform policies. Also, the rapid star growth may be inflated by marketing or bot activity, though the commit history and release cadence suggest genuine development.

projects.name: ncnn
projects.description: ncnn is a high-performance neural network inference framework optimized for the mobile platform

llmSummary.oneLiner: ncnn is a high-performance neural network inference framework optimized for mobile, embedded, and desktop deployment, with no third-party runtime dependencies and support for CPU and Vulkan GPU backends.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Deploying deep learning models efficiently on resource-constrained devices such as phones, embedded systems, and edge devices, without relying on heavy runtime dependencies.
llmSummary.whyNow: The project shows a sudden breakout with a 24-hour star delta of 8 and a 7-day delta of 36, but more importantly, the acceleration ratio is 2.4 (high), indicating a sharp increase in attention velocity. With 23,347 total stars and a recent release within 30 days, the project is experiencing a clear spike in interest, likely driven by new capabilities or community events.
llmSummary.whatChanged: A recent release (within 30 days) and active commits (within 7 days) suggest ongoing development. The README mentions support for new platforms like HarmonyOS and visionOS, which may have attracted new users. No single external trigger is obvious, but the combination of a new release and broad platform support likely contributed to the breakout.
llmSummary.whyTrending: ncnn is a mature, production-grade inference framework from Tencent, used in major apps like WeChat and QQ. Its recent updates expanding platform support (HarmonyOS, visionOS) and the growing demand for on-device AI inference are driving renewed interest.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: For developers building mobile or edge AI applications, ncnn offers a lightweight, high-performance alternative to TensorFlow Lite or Core ML, with direct conversion from PyTorch via pnnx. It's particularly valuable for cross-platform deployment without vendor lock-in.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward more efficient, platform-agnostic inference frameworks that can run on diverse hardware (CPU, Vulkan, RISC-V). Developers should consider ncnn for projects requiring low-latency inference on mobile or embedded devices, especially if they already use PyTorch.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Mobile app developers, embedded systems engineers, AI researchers deploying models on edge devices, and developers working with Android, iOS, HarmonyOS, or WebAssembly.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project has a high number of open issues (1180), which may indicate maintenance challenges or a large user base reporting bugs. The C++ API may have a steeper learning curve for Python-first developers, though Python bindings are available.

projects.name: ColossalAI
projects.description: Making large AI models cheaper, faster and more accessible

llmSummary.oneLiner: Colossal-AI is an open-source deep learning system that makes large AI models cheaper, faster, and more accessible through advanced parallelism and heterogeneous memory management.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Training and deploying large AI models (e.g., LLMs, video generation models) is prohibitively expensive and complex. Colossal-AI reduces cost and time by providing efficient distributed training and inference with minimal code changes.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has a sudden breakout with an acceleration of 6 (high), despite a low 7-day average star delta of -0.6. With 41,385 total stars and 4,505 forks, it's a well-established project that recently saw a spike in attention, possibly due to new benchmarks (B200 GPUs showing 50-70% throughput gains) or the release of Open-Sora and DeepSeek fine-tuning guides.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. However, recent README updates highlight new benchmarks on B200 GPUs, DeepSeek 671B fine-tuning, and Open-Sora video generation, which may have driven the breakout.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It offers a practical, cost-saving solution for training large models (e.g., 30% cost reduction with FP8), supports cutting-edge hardware (B200, H200), and provides turnkey examples for popular models like LLaMA, GPT, and Stable Diffusion.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Colossal-AI is a powerful tool for developers who need to scale model training or inference without deep distributed systems expertise. Its support for multiple parallelism strategies and heterogeneous memory makes it versatile for both research and production.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward democratizing large model training: developers can now achieve near-linear scaling on multi-GPU setups with minimal code changes, reducing the barrier to entry for building foundation models.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI researchers, ML engineers, and startups working on large-scale model training or inference, especially those using PyTorch and seeking cost-effective GPU utilization.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is backed by a company (HPC-AI Tech) that also offers paid cloud services, which may lead to feature prioritization for commercial users. Additionally, the rapid pace of change in AI infrastructure could make some parallelism strategies obsolete.

llmSummary.developerInsight: The rapid adoption of NextChat signals a shift toward unified, self-hosted AI interfaces that reduce dependency on single providers. The MCP support hints at a future where AI tools interoperate via standardized protocols, opening opportunities for plugin ecosystems.

llmSummary.riskNotes: The project relies on third-party API keys and services; users must manage their own keys. The rapid pace of features may introduce instability. No recent release tag could indicate a focus on bleeding-edge commits over stable releases.

modelDemoSignals.title: OmniGameArena: A Unified UE5 Benchmark for VLM Game Agents with Improvement Dynamics
modelDemoSignals.description: Vision-language model (VLM) agents are increasingly deployed in interactive game environments. Yet game benchmarks for VLM agents typically report a single first-attempt score per (agent, game) pair, focus on single-agent Solo play, and lack unified protocols for evaluating heterogeneous agent classes (commercial VLMs, open-weight VLMs, and specialized game policies) on the same footing. We address these gaps with OmniGameArena, a real-time benchmark of twelve newly built Unreal Engine 5 games s
modelDemoSignals.summary: Vision-language model (VLM) agents are increasingly deployed in interactive game environments. Yet game benchmarks for VLM agents typically report a single first-attempt score per (agent, game) pair, focus on single-agent Solo play, and lack unified protocols for evaluating heterogeneous agent classes (commercial VLMs, open-weight VLMs, and specialized game policies) on the same footing. We address these gaps with OmniGameArena, a real-time benchmark of twelve newly built Unreal Engine 5 games spanning Solo (7), PvP (3), and Coop (2) with unified action interfaces, and the Improvement Dynamics Curve (IDC), an agentic-reflection harness in which a tool-using reflector LLM autonomously refines a bounded skill prompt across multiple rounds. Beyond cold-start leaderboard scores, IDC exposes two additional observables for each (agent, game) pair: how the score evolves across reflection rounds, and how the learned skill behaves on held-out task variants. We report these observables for twelve VLM agents on the cold-start leaderboard and four top agents under IDC.
modelDemoSignals.author: Mingxian Lin, Shengju Qian, Yuqi Liu, Yi-Hua Huang, Yiyu Wang, Wei Huang, Yitang Li, Fan Zhang, Zeyu Hu, Lingting Zhu, Xin Wang, Xiaojuan Qi
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, agent, benchmark, agentic

modelDemoSignals.title: An Agency-Transferring Model-Free Policy Enhancement Technique
modelDemoSignals.description: Training reinforcement learning (RL) policies from scratch is costly: it requires careful reward and environment design, extensive tuning, and substantial computation. Yet many control problems already have a functional but suboptimal policy available as a baseline. modelDemoSignals.summary: Training reinforcement learning (RL) policies from scratch is costly: it requires careful reward and environment design, extensive tuning, and substantial computation. Yet many control problems already have a functional but suboptimal policy available as a baseline. This paper proposes a method for embedding such a baseline into the RL training process, simultaneously improving training efficiency relative to from-scratch methods and producing a learning policy that outperforms the baseline. At each step, the method arbitrates between the baseline policy and a trainable learning policy, initially relying strongly on the baseline policy and then progressively transferring agency to the learning policy. By the end of training, the learning policy is a standalone neural network that operates without baseline policy support. The paper formalizes what it means for the baseline policy to be functional: under this policy, the agent reaches a goal set and remains there with high probability. The proposed arbitration mechanism is designed to exploit this property during training, yielding high goal-reaching rates right from the beginning of training. A theoretical analysis provides a formal interpretation of this behavior under stated assumptions and extends it to the final baseline-free regime, where explicit lower bounds are derived for the goal-reaching probability of the standalone learning policy. Empirical results on continuous-control benchmarks show that the proposed method achieves returns that match or exceed those of competitive approaches, while maintaining the highest goal-reaching rates throughout training among the compared methods -- including in the final stage, where the learning policy operates without any baseline support.
modelDemoSignals.author: Anton Bolychev, Georgiy Malaniya, Sinan Ibrahim, Pavel Osinenko
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent, embedding, benchmark

modelDemoSignals.description: Language models, as multi-task learners, acquire a wide range of abilities during training. A fundamental question is how much task-specific data is needed to learn a given task. Answering this for natural language is difficult: tasks are hard to delineate and can confound one another. To rigorously investigate the relationship between data frequency and learnability, we turn to a controlled setting using formal languages induced from probabilistic finite automata. These serve as a methodologica
modelDemoSignals.summary: Language models, as multi-task learners, acquire a wide range of abilities during training. A fundamental question is how much task-specific data is needed to learn a given task. Answering this for natural language is difficult: tasks are hard to delineate and can confound one another. To rigorously investigate the relationship between data frequency and learnability, we turn to a controlled setting using formal languages induced from probabilistic finite automata. These serve as a methodological testbed to demonstrate that standard correlational evaluation practices are inherently flawed. To enable causal analysis, we introduce the binning semiring, an algebraic object that lets us control how often a targeted property occurs in a sampled corpus. We formulate the experimental pipeline as a causal graphical model and derive decomposed Kullback-Leibler divergence metrics to measure the learnability of specific sub-tasks. Our experiments show that evaluating learnability without causal intervention leads to incorrect conclusions due to confounders in correlational analysis, and serve as a warning about correlational pitfalls in natural-language settings.
modelDemoSignals.author: Vésteinn Snæbjarnarson, Anej Svete, Josef Valvoda, Reda Boumasmoud, Brian DuSell, Ryan Cotterell

modelDemoSignals.title: Rethinking the Divergence Regularization in LLM RL
modelDemoSignals.description: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a key component of post-training large language models (LLMs). In practice, LLM RL is often off-policy because of training-inference mismatch and policy staleness, making trust-region control essential for stable optimization. modelDemoSignals.summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a key component of post-training large language models (LLMs). In practice, LLM RL is often off-policy because of training-inference mismatch and policy staleness, making trust-region control essential for stable optimization. Mainstream methods such as PPO and GRPO approximate this control with a ratio-clipping mechanism, but the importance ratio can be a poor proxy for distributional shift in long-tailed vocabularies. Recent work such as DPPO addresses this mismatch by replacing ratio-based clipping with a divergence-based mask, yielding a trust region defined by the sampled token's absolute probability shift. However, DPPO still relies on a hard mask: once a token crosses the trust-region boundary in a harmful direction, its gradient is discarded rather than corrected. To address this, we propose Divergence Regularized Policy Optimization (DRPO), which replaces the hard mask with a smooth advantage-weighted quadratic regularizer on policy shift. DRPO preserves the same trust-region geometry as DPPO while inducing bounded, continuous gradient weights that attenuate diverging updates and provide corrective signals beyond the boundary. Experiments across model scales, architectures, and precision settings show that DRPO improves the stability and efficiency of LLM RL training.
modelDemoSignals.author: Jiarui Yao, Xiangxin Zhou, Penghui Qi, Wee Sun Lee, Liefeng Bo, Tianyu Pang
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm

modelDemoSignals.title: PTL-Diffusion: Manifold-Aware Diffusion with Periodic Terminal Laws
modelDemoSignals.description: Standard diffusion models typically use a single time-homogeneous Gaussian terminal distribution as the reference law for generation. While this choice is analytically convenient and empirically powerful, it provides little explicit structure for data concentrated near low-dimensional manifolds, where different regions of the data distribution may correspond to distinct local geometric or semantic factors. As a result, the reverse model must recover manifold-level structure almost entirely from
modelDemoSignals.summary: Standard diffusion models typically use a single time-homogeneous Gaussian terminal distribution as the reference law for generation. While this choice is analytically convenient and empirically powerful, it provides little explicit structure for data concentrated near low-dimensional manifolds, where different regions of the data distribution may correspond to distinct local geometric or semantic factors. As a result, the reverse model must recover manifold-level structure almost entirely from an unstructured terminal reference distribution. We propose PTL-Diffusion, a proof-of-concept diffusion framework whose forward noising process converges to a nonconstant periodic family of Gaussian terminal laws rather than to a single invariant law. Unlike a phase-conditioned DDPM, where phase information only enters the denoising network while the forward process remains unchanged, PTL-Diffusion embeds phase structure directly into the forward noising dynamics. The proposed construction remains close to standard denoising diffusion models: for a periodically forced Ornstein--Uhlenbeck-type forward process, we derive closed-form forward marginals, the limiting periodic Gaussian terminal family, and explicit Gaussian reverse posteriors, enabling standard noise-prediction training. We also introduce an invariant-average regularization term coupling the phase-conditioned reverse dynamics through the averaged periodic reference law. Experiments on torus and cylinder point-cloud benchmarks and the Olivetti face dataset show that PTL-Diffusion improves manifold-level distributional matching over matched DDPM baselines, reducing phase-conditioned errors, feature-space covariance errors, and nearest-neighbour manifold distances. These results suggest structured terminal reference laws as a promising direction, while motivating more expressive phase constructions and larger-scale evaluations.
modelDemoSignals.author: Danqi Zhuang, Jisui Huang, Xiaoyue Xi, Andrew Kiggins, Xiaojie Wang, Ke Chen, Yue Wu
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: rag, benchmark, evaluation

aihotHighlights.summary: 确认，Claude Mythos 将在接下来几小时内揭晓。 【引用 @steph_palazzolo】： 独家：一个名为 Claude Fable 的精简版 Mythos 今天推出。它价格昂贵--是 Opus 的两倍--但或许不像人们从最初 Mythos 定价（Opus 的 5 倍）所想的那样昂贵。 更多内容及 Apple WWDC 见 AI Agenda： https：//www.theinformation.com/newsletters/ai-agenda/anthropics-mythos-coming-today-apple-pursues-modest-goals-siri-revamp

aihotHighlights.title: Google DeepMind 发布 Gemma 4 12B：统一的无编码器多模态模型
aihotHighlights.summary: Gemma 4 12B 是 Google DeepMind 最新推出的中等规模多模态模型，采用无编码器统一架构，原生支持音频输入。其基准测试性能接近 26B MoE 模型，但内存占用不到一半，仅需 16GB 显存或统一内存即可在消费级笔记本上本地运行。模型内置多 token 预测（MTP）drafter 以降低延迟，基于 Apache 2.0 开源许可发布，已累计超过 1.5 亿次下载。

aihotHighlights.title: 全新汽车品牌AIVA发布，火山引擎助力打造AI汽车新体验
aihotHighlights.summary: 由赛力斯、宁德时代等多方产业资本组建的AI出行品牌AIVA正式发布。火山引擎提供豆包大模型、智能座舱等技术服务。概念车AIVA Origin Concept亮相，首款量产车AIVA ME7将于2026年内亮相，全系覆盖20万元以上市场。AIVA提出"AI定义汽车"路径，让汽车成为具身AI生命体。火山引擎副总裁表示，人与汽车的关系将实现交互、智能、感受三方面根本转变。未来双方将围绕AI交互、智能体验、情感陪伴深度共创。

aihotHighlights.title: 百度搭子DuMate获中国信通院企业级Claw能力评估最高4+级
aihotHighlights.summary: 2026年6月，百度智能云旗下百度搭子DuMate V3.4.0通过中国信通院「可信AI-企业级Claw能力评估」，获最高评级4+级，为国内首批。评估依据《智能助理智能体（Claw）技术和应用要求 第2部分 企业级Claw能力》（AIIA/T 0295-2026），覆盖智能体、工程化部署、服务、业务融合、运行管理五大能力域。百度搭子支持多智能体分工协作、容器化批量部署、多租户隔离与三级差异化授权、企业知识库接入、全链路日志审计等能力，企业级智能助手服务能力获国家级权威认可。

aihotHighlights.title: 腾讯混元发布UniRL：统一多模态强化学习基础设施
aihotHighlights.summary: 腾讯混元推出UniRL，一个支持统一多模态模型的强化学习基础设施，并发布两个新算法DRPO和Flow-DPPO。UniRL通过单个后训练循环（生成→评分→优势→更新→同步）覆盖扩散/流匹配模型、LLM/VLM及统一多模态模型（如Hunyuan-Image 3和Bagel）。模型与算法作为独立轴，可实现模型×算法的组合覆盖。框架支持可插拔rollout引擎（训练侧/SGLang/vLLM-Omni）、FSDP2分片和三种部署模式。FlowDPPO针对流/扩散模型引入基于精确散度的信任域策略优化；DRPO为LLM RL提供平滑的优势加权二次正则化方法。代码已开源。

aihotHighlights.title: 火山引擎TRAE Work企业版正式上线，面向全员提供AI办公平台
aihotHighlights.summary: 火山引擎将TRAE Solo品牌升级为TRAE Work企业版，发布面向企业的AI办公平台。平台提供Work和Code两种模式：Work模式面向产品、运营、市场等非技术岗位，支持上传.pptx、.xlsx、图片等多种格式混合输入直接输出PPT或文档，支持语音讨论自动整理结构化纪要，支持按天或按周自动运行的数据整理和报告生成；Code模式面向开发者和业务同学，可用自然语言描述需求直接生成页面或小应用。桌面端、网页端和移动端多端同步。企业后台可统一配置模型、用量限额、上传内部文档，沙箱机制隔离执行，支持命令黑名单、MCP白名单、内容安全策略，关键操作可审计。同时发起TRAE Enterprise Fellow招募计划。

aihotHighlights.title: Kimi 预测全部104场世界杯比赛：德国或被低估
aihotHighlights.summary: Kimi 利用 Agent Swarm 系统并行协调300个子智能体，分析战术、球员状态、伤病、赛程、天气、赔率等因素，预测2026年美加墨世界杯全部104场比赛，并发布每轮赛前预测和赛后回顾。模型层融合了 Elo/FIFA 强度、Poisson 进球分布、xG/xT 指标、蒙特卡洛模拟等方法。预测结果显示西班牙和法国为头号热门，但德国夺冠概率可能被市场低估：模型基线估计约11.0%，校准估计约11.3%，而部分市场隐含概率仅约7.4%，正向偏差约+3.6个百分点。该判断基于多分析链交叉验证，可能源于对德国近两届小组出局的近因偏差以及纳格尔斯曼高位压迫体系与穆西亚拉/维尔茨新创造轴的复苏信号。

aihotHighlights.title: 可灵AI×候鸟300发起AI主义新浪潮AIGC影像大赛
aihotHighlights.summary: 可灵AI与候鸟300联合发起AIGC影像大赛。线下大赛6月16-26日在阿那亚海边举办，评审包括李少红、陈明昊、焦雄屏等影视与AI专家及美院教授，设10万奖金+超200万灵感值，结果6月27日晚揭晓。线上大赛6月22日前投稿可角逐"最受关注大奖"，获奖者受邀参加6月27日颁奖。参赛要求：视频≥50%由可灵AI生成，官网投稿填表并带话题分享。

aihotHighlights.title: 一个Agent如何通过链式调用两个HuggingFace Space构建3D巴黎画廊
aihotHighlights.summary: 一个编码Agent调用HuggingFace上的两个Space，从零构建了展示巴黎地标3D高斯散点图的交互式画廊。Agent先用ideogram-ai/ideogram4生成每个纪念碑的黑色背景图像，再通过VAST-AI/TripoSplat从单张图像重建3D高斯散点（.ply），自动完成坐标系校正、取景、压缩为.ksplat（体积缩小约3倍），并构建基于Three.js的滚动切换、拖拽旋转查看器，最终部署为静态Space。整个过程无需客户端库，每个Space通过`agents.md`暴露可调用API。
aihotHighlights.description: 一个编码Agent调用HuggingFace上的两个Space，从零构建了展示巴黎地标3D高斯散点图的交互式画廊。Agent先用ideogram-ai/ideogram4生成每个纪念碑的黑色背景图像，再通过VAST-AI/TripoSplat从单张图像重建3D高斯散点（.ply），自动完成坐标系校正、取景、压缩为.ksplat（体积缩小约3倍），并构建基于Three.js的滚动切换、拖拽旋转查看器，最终部署为静态Space。整个过程无需客户端库，每个Space通过`agents.md`暴露可调用API。

llmSummary.whatItIs: Apple's Core AI framework is a new on-device AI/ML framework for iOS/macOS developers, announced at WWDC 2026.
llmSummary.whyNow: The framework was just announced and is generating significant developer interest on Hacker News (339 upvotes, 98 comments) and AIHot, indicating strong curiosity about Apple's AI strategy.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: iOS/macOS developers, ML engineers building on-device AI features, product managers evaluating Apple ecosystem AI capabilities.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Apple's entry into on-device AI frameworks could shift mobile AI development, but adoption depends on documentation quality and performance vs. alternatives like Core ML.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Developers should evaluate Core AI for new projects requiring on-device inference, but wait for benchmarks and community feedback before committing.
llmSummary.riskNotes: New framework with unknown API stability, performance, and adoption. Hype from WWDC may not reflect real-world utility. No production apps yet.

items.title: Claude Mythos 即将发布，Fable 精简版同日登场
items.description: 确认，Claude Mythos 将在接下来几小时内揭晓。 【引用 @steph_palazzolo】： 独家：一个名为 Claude Fable 的精简版 Mythos 今天推出。它价格昂贵--是 Opus 的两倍--但或许不像人们从最初 Mythos 定价（Opus 的 5 倍）所想的那样昂贵。 更多内容及 Apple WWDC 见 AI Agenda： https：//www.theinformation.com/newsletters/ai-agenda/anthropics-mythos-coming-today-apple-pursues-modest-goals-siri-revamp

items.title: Google DeepMind 发布 Gemma 4 12B：统一的无编码器多模态模型
items.description: Gemma 4 12B 是 Google DeepMind 最新推出的中等规模多模态模型，采用无编码器统一架构，原生支持音频输入。其基准测试性能接近 26B MoE 模型，但内存占用不到一半，仅需 16GB 显存或统一内存即可在消费级笔记本上本地运行。模型内置多 token 预测（MTP）drafter 以降低延迟，基于 Apache 2.0 开源许可发布，已累计超过 1.5 亿次下载。

items.title: 全新汽车品牌AIVA发布，火山引擎助力打造AI汽车新体验
items.description: 由赛力斯、宁德时代等多方产业资本组建的AI出行品牌AIVA正式发布。火山引擎提供豆包大模型、智能座舱等技术服务。概念车AIVA Origin Concept亮相，首款量产车AIVA ME7将于2026年内亮相，全系覆盖20万元以上市场。AIVA提出"AI定义汽车"路径，让汽车成为具身AI生命体。火山引擎副总裁表示，人与汽车的关系将实现交互、智能、感受三方面根本转变。未来双方将围绕AI交互、智能体验、情感陪伴深度共创。

items.title: 百度搭子DuMate获中国信通院企业级Claw能力评估最高4+级
items.description: 2026年6月，百度智能云旗下百度搭子DuMate V3.4.0通过中国信通院「可信AI-企业级Claw能力评估」，获最高评级4+级，为国内首批。评估依据《智能助理智能体（Claw）技术和应用要求 第2部分 企业级Claw能力》（AIIA/T 0295-2026），覆盖智能体、工程化部署、服务、业务融合、运行管理五大能力域。百度搭子支持多智能体分工协作、容器化批量部署、多租户隔离与三级差异化授权、企业知识库接入、全链路日志审计等能力，企业级智能助手服务能力获国家级权威认可。

Claude Mythos 即将发布，Fable 精简版同日登场
items.description: 确认，Claude Mythos 将在接下来几小时内揭晓。 【引用 @steph_palazzolo】： 独家：一个名为 Claude Fable 的精简版 Mythos 今天推出。它价格昂贵--是 Opus 的两倍--但或许不像人们从最初 Mythos 定价（Opus 的 5 倍）所想的那样昂贵。 更多内容及 Apple WWDC 见 AI Agenda： https：//www.theinformation.com/newsletters/ai-agenda/anthropics-mythos-coming-today-apple-pursues-modest-goals-siri-revamp

llmSummary.whoShouldCare: Developers building LLM-powered tools, platform engineers managing API costs, and teams adopting MCP or agent-based architectures.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Directly reduces operational costs for LLM API usage, making it attractive for startups and enterprises scaling AI features.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Provides a drop-in solution to cut token spend without code changes, relevant for anyone using LLM APIs in development or production.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Star growth may reflect hype rather than production adoption; token reduction claims depend on workload patterns; single-binary approach may limit extensibility.

items.title: Claude Mythos 即将发布，Fable 精简版同日登场
items.summary: 确认，Claude Mythos 将在接下来几小时内揭晓。 【引用 @steph_palazzolo】： 独家：一个名为 Claude Fable 的精简版 Mythos 今天推出。它价格昂贵--是 Opus 的两倍--但或许不像人们从最初 Mythos 定价（Opus 的 5 倍）所想的那样昂贵。 更多内容及 Apple WWDC 见 AI Agenda： https：//www.theinformation.com/newsletters/ai-agenda/anthropics-mythos-coming-today-apple-pursues-modest-goals-siri-revamp

trendEntities.title: Google DeepMind 发布 Gemma 4 12B：统一的无编码器多模态模型
normalizedKeys: title:google deepmind 发布 gemma 4 12b 统一的无编码器多模态模型

items.title: Google DeepMind 发布 Gemma 4 12B：统一的无编码器多模态模型
items.summary: Gemma 4 12B 是 Google DeepMind 最新推出的中等规模多模态模型，采用无编码器统一架构，原生支持音频输入。其基准测试性能接近 26B MoE 模型，但内存占用不到一半，仅需 16GB 显存或统一内存即可在消费级笔记本上本地运行。模型内置多 token 预测（MTP）drafter 以降低延迟，基于 Apache 2.0 开源许可发布，已累计超过 1.5 亿次下载。

trendEntities.title: 全新汽车品牌AIVA发布，火山引擎助力打造AI汽车新体验
normalizedKeys: title:全新汽车品牌aiva发布 火山引擎助力打造ai汽车新体验

items.title: 全新汽车品牌AIVA发布，火山引擎助力打造AI汽车新体验
items.summary: 由赛力斯、宁德时代等多方产业资本组建的AI出行品牌AIVA正式发布。火山引擎提供豆包大模型、智能座舱等技术服务。概念车AIVA Origin Concept亮相，首款量产车AIVA ME7将于2026年内亮相，全系覆盖20万元以上市场。AIVA提出"AI定义汽车"路径，让汽车成为具身AI生命体。火山引擎副总裁表示，人与汽车的关系将实现交互、智能、感受三方面根本转变。未来双方将围绕AI交互、智能体验、情感陪伴深度共创。

trendEntities.title: 百度搭子DuMate获中国信通院企业级Claw能力评估最高4+级
normalizedKeys: title:百度搭子dumate获中国信通院企业级claw能力评估最高4 级

items.title: 百度搭子DuMate获中国信通院企业级Claw能力评估最高4+级
items.summary: 2026年6月，百度智能云旗下百度搭子DuMate V3.4.0通过中国信通院「可信AI-企业级Claw能力评估」，获最高评级4+级，为国内首批。评估依据《智能助理智能体（Claw）技术和应用要求 第2部分 企业级Claw能力》（AIIA/T 0295-2026），覆盖智能体、工程化部署、服务、业务融合、运行管理五大能力域。百度搭子支持多智能体分工协作、容器化批量部署、多租户隔离与三级差异化授权、企业知识库接入、全链路日志审计等能力，企业级智能助手服务能力获国家级权威认可。

trendEntities.title: 腾讯混元发布UniRL：统一多模态强化学习基础设施
normalizedKeys: title:腾讯混元发布unirl 统一多模态强化学习基础设施

items.title: 腾讯混元发布UniRL：统一多模态强化学习基础设施
items.summary: 腾讯混元推出UniRL，一个支持统一多模态模型的强化学习基础设施，并发布两个新算法DRPO和Flow-DPPO。UniRL通过单个后训练循环（生成→评分→优势→更新→同步）覆盖扩散/流匹配模型、LLM/VLM及统一多模态模型（如Hunyuan-Image 3和Bagel）。模型与算法作为独立轴，可实现模型×算法的组合覆盖。框架支持可插拔rollout引擎（训练侧/SGLang/vLLM-Omni）、FSDP2分片和三种部署模式。FlowDPPO针对流/扩散模型引入基于精确散度的信任域策略优化；DRPO为LLM RL提供平滑的优势加权二次正则化方法。代码已开源。

trendEntities.title: 火山引擎TRAE Work企业版正式上线，面向全员提供AI办公平台
normalizedKeys: title:火山引擎trae work企业版正式上线 面向全员提供ai办公平台

items.title: 火山引擎TRAE Work企业版正式上线，面向全员提供AI办公平台
items.summary: 火山引擎将TRAE Solo品牌升级为TRAE Work企业版，发布面向企业的AI办公平台。平台提供Work和Code两种模式：Work模式面向产品、运营、市场等非技术岗位，支持上传.pptx、.xlsx、图片等多种格式混合输入直接输出PPT或文档，支持语音讨论自动整理结构化纪要，支持按天或按周自动运行的数据整理和报告生成；Code模式面向开发者和业务同学，可用自然语言描述需求直接生成页面或小应用。桌面端、网页端和移动端多端同步。企业后台可统一配置模型、用量限额、上传内部文档，沙箱机制隔离执行，支持命令黑名单、MCP白名单、内容安全策略，关键操作可审计。同时发起TRAE Enterprise Fellow招募计划。

trendEntities.title: Kimi 预测全部104场世界杯比赛：德国或被低估
normalizedKeys: title:kimi 预测全部104场世界杯比赛 德国或被低估

items.title: Kimi 预测全部104场世界杯比赛：德国或被低估
items.summary: Kimi 利用 Agent Swarm 系统并行协调300个子智能体，分析战术、球员状态、伤病、赛程、天气、赔率等因素，预测2026年美加墨世界杯全部104场比赛，并发布每轮赛前预测和赛后回顾。模型层融合了 Elo/FIFA 强度、Poisson 进球分布、xG/xT 指标、蒙特卡洛模拟等方法。预测结果显示西班牙和法国为头号热门，但德国夺冠概率可能被市场低估：模型基线估计约11.0%，校准估计约11.3%，而部分市场隐含概率仅约7.4%，正向偏差约+3.6个百分点。该判断基于多分析链交叉验证，可能源于对德国近两届小组出局的近因偏差以及纳格尔斯曼高位压迫体系与穆西亚拉/维尔茨新创造轴的复苏信号。

trendEntities.title: 可灵AI×候鸟300发起AI主义新浪潮AIGC影像大赛
normalizedKeys: title:可灵ai 候鸟300发起ai主义新浪潮aigc影像大赛

items.title: 可灵AI×候鸟300发起AI主义新浪潮AIGC影像大赛
items.summary: 可灵AI与候鸟300联合发起AIGC影像大赛。线下大赛6月16-26日在阿那亚海边举办，评审包括李少红、陈明昊、焦雄屏等影视与AI专家及美院教授，设10万奖金+超200万灵感值，结果6月27日晚揭晓。线上大赛6月22日前投稿可角逐"最受关注大奖"，获奖者受邀参加6月27日颁奖。参赛要求：视频≥50%由可灵AI生成，官网投稿填表并带话题分享。

trendEntities.title: 一个Agent如何通过链式调用两个HuggingFace Space构建3D巴黎画廊
normalizedKeys: title:一个agent如何通过链式调用两个huggingface space构建3d巴黎画廊

items.title: 一个Agent如何通过链式调用两个HuggingFace Space构建3D巴黎画廊
items.summary: 一个编码Agent调用HuggingFace上的两个Space，从零构建了展示巴黎地标3D高斯散点图的交互式画廊。Agent先用ideogram-ai/ideogram4生成每个纪念碑的黑色背景图像，再通过VAST-AI/TripoSplat从单张图像重建3D高斯散点（.ply），自动完成坐标系校正、取景、压缩为.ksplat（体积缩小约3倍），并构建基于Three.js的滚动切换、拖拽旋转查看器，最终部署为静态Space。整个过程无需客户端库，每个Space通过`agents.md`暴露可调用API。

items.title: 台湾考虑限制AI芯片对华出口以配合美国
items.summary: 据知情人士透露，台湾当局正考虑对AI芯片出口中国大陆实施更严格的管制，以进一步与美国出口限制措施对齐。此举旨在遏制半导体走私，但可能招致北京方面的谴责。

trendEntities.title: 仅凭一份文档，Qwen3.7-Max 从 0 交付双端应用
normalizedKeys: title:仅凭一份文档 qwen3 7 max 从 0 交付双端应用

items.title: 仅凭一份文档，Qwen3.7-Max 从 0 交付双端应用
items.summary: 在无设计稿和后端代码的条件下，Qwen3.7-Max 仅凭一份约 15 万字的产品调研文档，于隔离环境中全自动完成移动端与 Web 端两套真实应用从 0 到 1 交付，单端耗时约 4 小时，中途无人工接管。模型不具备图像理解能力，通过像素坐标反推布局约束实现界面还原。实验采用"分阶段注入约束→逐层验收→带错纠正"的闭环控制系统：任务拆分为规划、架构、编码等阶段，验收覆盖静态检查、编译自检（0 error）、路由完整性（Web 端 34 条路由全部可达）、功能扫描及真机冷启动冒烟。失败时错误文本自动注入下一轮重试，使模型数小时内收敛。移动端产出可安装 APK，Web 端 typecheck 与构建均通过。

sourceItems.summary: 在无设计稿和后端代码的条件下，Qwen3.7-Max 仅凭一份约 15 万字的产品调研文档，于隔离环境中全自动完成移动端与 Web 端两套真实应用从 0 到 1 交付，单端耗时约 4 小时，中途无人工接管。模型不具备图像理解能力，通过像素坐标反推布局约束实现界面还原。实验采用"分阶段注入约束→逐层验收→带错纠正"的闭环控制系统：任务拆分为规划、架构、编码等阶段，验收覆盖静态检查、编译自检（0 error）、路由完整性（Web 端 34 条路由全部可达）、功能扫描及真机冷启动冒烟。失败时错误文本自动注入下一轮重试，使模型数小时内收敛。移动端产出可安装 APK，Web 端 typecheck 与构建均通过。

trendEntities.title: NeuroBait：微调AI助手，为ADHD大脑点燃多巴胺火花
normalizedKeys: title:neurobait 微调ai助手 为adhd大脑点燃多巴胺火花

items.title: NeuroBait：微调AI助手，为ADHD大脑点燃多巴胺火花
items.summary: NeuroBait是基于Google gemma-3-12b-it微调的AI对话助手，旨在帮助ADHD患者克服"知道该做什么但无法开始"的执行功能障碍。采用16-bit LoRA（r=16， alpha=16）在Unsloth上训练3个epoch，学习率2e-4，最大序列长度2048，使用单张H100 80GB GPU。数据集为基于真实ADHD场景手工合成的少量数据。部署于Hugging Face Space（ZeroGPU），使用Gradio和标准transformers+peft，运行时以4-bit NF4加载基础模型并应用LoRA适配器。NeuroBait不生成待办清单，而是根据用户上下文给出3-6句温暖流畅的回复，引导用户找到一件微小可立即执行的动作，从而激发多巴胺、降低启动阻力。

trendEntities.title: NVIDIA cuTile Python 教程：在 Colab 中构建用于向量加法、矩阵加法和矩阵乘法的 Tiled GPU 内核
normalizedKeys: title:nvidia cutile python 教程 在 colab 中构建用于向量加法 矩阵加法和矩阵乘法的 tiled gpu 内核

items.title: NVIDIA cuTile Python 教程：在 Colab 中构建用于向量加法、矩阵加法和矩阵乘法的 Tiled GPU 内核
items.summary: 该教程基于 NVIDIA cuTile Python 实现了分块 GPU 内核编程工作流，在 Colab 环境中配置 GPU、驱动、CUDA 及 cuTile 可用性后，分别构建了 tiled 向量加法、矩阵加法和矩阵乘法核函数，并以 PyTorch 作为回退保持 notebook 可执行。每一步均通过 PyTorch 验证结果正确性，并基准测试了各阶段的中位运行时间。
items.description: 该教程基于 NVIDIA cuTile Python 实现了分块 GPU 内核编程工作流，在 Colab 环境中配置 GPU、驱动、CUDA 及 cuTile 可用性后，分别构建了 tiled 向量加法、矩阵加法和矩阵乘法核函数，并以 PyTorch 作为回退保持 notebook 可执行。每一步均通过 PyTorch 验证结果正确性，并基准测试了各阶段的中位运行时间。

trendEntities.title: 硅基流动联合CodeWhale推出DeepSeek V4终端最佳性价比组合
normalizedKeys: title:硅基流动联合codewhale推出deepseek v4终端最佳性价比组合

items.title: 硅基流动联合CodeWhale推出DeepSeek V4终端最佳性价比组合
items.summary: 硅基流动宣布，通过V4-Pro（质量）与V4-Flash（速度）两行配置，即可在终端获得DeepSeek V4的最佳性价比组合。专为DeepSeek V4构建的终端编码智能体CodeWhale现已内置SiliconFlow。CodeWhale具备流式推理（显示思考过程）、自动路由（根据任务复杂度切换模型与思考深度）、零漂移（通过书面宪法为每轮排序权威，保持V4定向）以及自我改进（V4协助编写框架，框架提升后每个会话更强大）等特性。

items.title: 中国准备2950亿美元计划资助全国AI基础设施建设
items.summary: 中国计划在未来五年投入约2万亿元人民币（约2950亿美元）建设全国数据中心，以推动国内AI产业发展并超越美国。该投资将覆盖数据中心基础设施的大规模建设，为北京在关键技术领域的雄心提供资金支持。

trendEntities.title: AI 编程独角兽 Cursor 欧洲总部落子伦敦，SpaceX 手握 600 亿美元收购选择权
normalizedKeys: title:ai 编程独角兽 cursor 欧洲总部落子伦敦 spacex 手握 600 亿美元收购选择权

items.title: AI 编程独角兽 Cursor 欧洲总部落子伦敦，SpaceX 手握 600 亿美元收购选择权
items.summary: Cursor 将欧洲总部设在伦敦，计划招聘约 200 名员工，并在巴黎、慕尼黑等地开设小型办事处。SpaceX 拥有以 600 亿美元收购 Cursor 的选择权，或支付 100 亿美元开展全新合作。Cursor 目前 B2B 年化营收约 26 亿美元，客户包括英国航空、英国石油、诺基亚等。公司强调数据留存欧洲本地以满足监管合规，其平台支持用户用自然语言生成代码，主打模型中立，竞争对手包括 GitHub Copilot、OpenAI 和谷歌的同类产品。

trendEntities.title: 小米 MiMo 与 TileRT 联合发布 UltraSpeed 模式，1T 模型输出突破 1000 tokens/s
normalizedKeys: title:小米 mimo 与 tilert 联合发布 ultraspeed 模式 1t 模型输出突破 1000 tokens s

items.title: 小米 MiMo 与 TileRT 联合发布 UltraSpeed 模式，1T 模型输出突破 1000 tokens/s
items.summary: 小米 MiMo 与 TileRT 联合发布 MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed 模式，使 1T 参数旗舰模型输出速度首次突破 1000 tokens/s。模型侧采用 FP4 混合量化（仅量化 MoE Expert）与 DFlash 块级 masked 并行推测解码（coding 场景平均接受长度 6.30 tokens）；系统侧 TileRT 引入常驻内核引擎与异构流水线协作。API 限时开放（2026 年 6 月 9 日至 23 日），定价为 MiMo-V2.5-Pro 的 3 倍，速度提升约 10 倍。FP4 权重与 DFlash 模型 checkpoint 已开源至 HuggingFace。
items.description: 小米 MiMo 与 TileRT 联合发布 MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed 模式，使 1T 参数旗舰模型输出速度首次突破 1000 tokens/s。模型侧采用 FP4 混合量化（仅量化 MoE Expert）与 DFlash 块级 masked 并行推测解码（coding 场景平均接受长度 6.30 tokens）；系统侧 TileRT 引入常驻内核引擎与异构流水线协作。API 限时开放（2026 年 6 月 9 日至 23 日），定价为 MiMo-V2.5-Pro 的 3 倍，速度提升约 10 倍。FP4 权重与 DFlash 模型 checkpoint 已开源至 HuggingFace。

trendEntities.title: OpenAI 秘密提交 IPO 申请，奥特曼旗下 Tools for Humanity 裁员
normalizedKeys: title:openai 秘密提交 ipo 申请 奥特曼旗下 tools for humanity 裁员

items.title: OpenAI 秘密提交 IPO 申请，奥特曼旗下 Tools for Humanity 裁员
items.summary: OpenAI 近日秘密提交 IPO 申请。其 CEO 山姆·奥特曼旗下的 Tools for Humanity 公司正裁员，该公司因虹膜扫描项目 World 及加密货币 Worldcoin 知名，投后估值 25 亿美元，获 Andreessen Horowitz 等投资。因营收困境，公司缩减规模。海外监管方面，肯尼亚以隐私和金融风险为由叫停运营，韩国因违反隐私法规罚款 83 万美元。

trendEntities.title: Elon Musk 详解 SpaceX AI1 轨道 AI 数据中心卫星方案
normalizedKeys: title:elon musk 详解 spacex ai1 轨道 ai 数据中心卫星方案

items.title: Elon Musk 详解 SpaceX AI1 轨道 AI 数据中心卫星方案
items.summary: Elon Musk 首次详细解释 SpaceX 的 AI1 轨道 AI 数据中心卫星：峰值功率 150 kW，持续计算功率约 120 kW，相当于一个 NVIDIA GB300 机架；太阳能板效率 250 W/m2；双面散热器排热 1，400 W/m2。通过激光链路实现约 1 Tbps 互联，低轨 600-800 km 高度往返延迟 6-8 ms。由 Starship 发射，计划部署多达百万颗卫星，2027 年底前实现量产。近地目标为吉瓦级轨道 AI 算力，长期向太瓦级推进。

trendEntities.title: 开源工具 Tokei：在菜单栏实时监控 AI coding agent 的 token 用量与成本
normalizedKeys: title:开源工具 tokei 在菜单栏实时监控 ai coding agent 的 token 用量与成本

items.title: 开源工具 Tokei：在菜单栏实时监控 AI coding agent 的 token 用量与成本
items.summary: Berry Xia 推荐开源工具 Tokei，这是一个 macOS 菜单栏小工具，只读本地日志、零网络调用，30 秒自动刷新，实时监控 Claude Code、Grok CLI、Aider、OpenCode 等 8 个主流 AI coding agent 的 token 用量、实时成本与性能数据，并附每日图表、周热力图和年度 Wrapped。支持私人 Git 多设备同步、价格表本地覆盖，闲置过久会提醒休息。引用推文指出，token 消耗情况可作为深度使用 AI 的面试亮点，数据全程私有，零侵入，无需任何认证。

trendEntities.title: 两部门：到2026年底人形机器人等重点产品完成应用验证并常态部署
normalizedKeys: title:两部门 到2026年底人形机器人等重点产品完成应用验证并常态部署

items.title: 两部门：到2026年底人形机器人等重点产品完成应用验证并常态部署
items.summary: 工信部、国资委6月8日联合发布通知，目标到2026年底，人形机器人等重点产品在代表性场景完成应用验证并开启常态部署，形成百个以上高价值场景，万台级规模落地。要求各省级地区选取不少于20个场景单元（覆盖两类领域），央企不少于10个。围绕打造实景实训空间、组建创新应用联合体、攻关作业技能、加强验证部署、强化要素保障、凝练经验等六大任务展开，鼓励"人形机器人即服务"等商业创新。

trendEntities.title: FrontierCode 基准测试：AI 编程评估新标准--维护者审核通过率最高仅 13.4%
normalizedKeys: title:frontiercode 基准测试 ai 编程评估新标准 维护者审核通过率最高仅 13 4

items.title: FrontierCode 基准测试：AI 编程评估新标准--维护者审核通过率最高仅 13.4%
items.summary: Cognition 发布 FrontierCode 基准测试，重新定义 AI 编程评估：由 20 多位顶级开源维护者手工制作 150 个任务（每个耗时 40+ 小时），依据 3000 多条规则判断维护者是否愿意合并代码。该基准指出 SWE-Bench 等超半数通过测试的代码实为不可维护的垃圾。结果中 Claude Opus 4.8 在最高难度档获 13.4%，GPT-5.5 为 6.3%，其余模型 1%-5%。这意味着即便最强模型，近九成代码仍无法通过有经验维护者审核。

items.title: OmniGameArena: A Unified UE5 Benchmark for VLM Game Agents with Improvement Dynamics
items.description: Vision-language model (VLM) agents are increasingly deployed in interactive game environments. Yet game benchmarks for VLM agents typically report a single first-attempt score per (agent, game) pair, focus on single-agent Solo play, and lack unified protocols for evaluating heterogeneous agent classes (commercial VLMs, open-weight VLMs, and specialized game policies) on the same footing. We address these gaps with OmniGameArena, a real-time benchmark of twelve newly built Unreal Engine 5 games s
items.summary: Vision-language model (VLM) agents are increasingly deployed in interactive game environments. Yet game benchmarks for VLM agents typically report a single first-attempt score per (agent, game) pair, focus on single-agent Solo play, and lack unified protocols for evaluating heterogeneous agent classes (commercial VLMs, open-weight VLMs, and specialized game policies) on the same footing. We address these gaps with OmniGameArena, a real-time benchmark of twelve newly built Unreal Engine 5 games spanning Solo (7), PvP (3), and Coop (2) with unified action interfaces, and the Improvement Dynamics Curve (IDC), an agentic-reflection harness in which a tool-using reflector LLM autonomously refines a bounded skill prompt across multiple rounds. Beyond cold-start leaderboard scores, IDC exposes two additional observables for each (agent, game) pair: how the score evolves across reflection rounds, and how the learned skill behaves on held-out task variants. We report these observables for twelve VLM agents on the cold-start leaderboard and four top agents under IDC.
items.author: Mingxian Lin, Shengju Qian, Yuqi Liu, Yi-Hua Huang, Yiyu Wang, Wei Huang, Yitang Li, Fan Zhang, Zeyu Hu, Lingting Zhu, Xin Wang, Xiaojuan Qi
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, agent, benchmark, agentic

trendEntities.title: An Agency-Transferring Model-Free Policy Enhancement Technique
normalizedKeys: title:an agency transferring model free policy enhancement technique

items.title: An Agency-Transferring Model-Free Policy Enhancement Technique
items.description: Training reinforcement learning (RL) policies from scratch is costly: it requires careful reward and environment design, extensive tuning, and substantial computation. Yet many control problems already have a functional but suboptimal policy available as a baseline. This paper proposes a method for embedding such a baseline into the RL training process, simultaneously improving training efficiency relative to from-scratch methods and producing a learning policy that outperforms the baseline. items.summary: Training reinforcement learning (RL) policies from scratch is costly: it requires careful reward and environment design, extensive tuning, and substantial computation. Yet many control problems already have a functional but suboptimal policy available as a baseline. This paper proposes a method for embedding such a baseline into the RL training process, simultaneously improving training efficiency relative to from-scratch methods and producing a learning policy that outperforms the baseline. At each step, the method arbitrates between the baseline policy and a trainable learning policy, initially relying strongly on the baseline policy and then progressively transferring agency to the learning policy. By the end of training, the learning policy is a standalone neural network that operates without baseline policy support. The paper formalizes what it means for the baseline policy to be functional: under this policy, the agent reaches a goal set and remains there with high probability. The proposed arbitration mechanism is designed to exploit this property during training, yielding high goal-reaching rates right from the beginning of training. A theoretical analysis provides a formal interpretation of this behavior under stated assumptions and extends it to the final baseline-free regime, where explicit lower bounds are derived for the goal-reaching probability of the standalone learning policy. Empirical results on continuous-control benchmarks show that the proposed method achieves returns that match or exceed those of competitive approaches, while maintaining the highest goal-reaching rates throughout training among the compared methods -- including in the final stage, where the learning policy operates without any baseline support.
items.author: Anton Bolychev, Georgiy Malaniya, Sinan Ibrahim, Pavel Osinenko
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent, embedding, benchmark

At each step, the method arbitrates between the baseline policy and a trainable learning policy, initially relying strongly on the baseline policy and then progressively transferring agency to the learning policy. By the end of training, the learning policy is a standalone neural network that operates without baseline policy support. The paper formalizes what it means for the baseline policy to be functional: under this policy, the agent reaches a goal set and remains there with high probability. The proposed arbitration mechanism is designed to exploit this property during training, yielding high goal-reaching rates right from the beginning of training. A theoretical analysis provides a formal interpretation of this behavior under stated assumptions and extends it to the final baseline-free regime, where explicit lower bounds are derived for the goal-reaching probability of the standalone learning policy. Empirical results on continuous-control benchmarks show that the proposed method achieves returns that match or exceed those of competitive approaches, while maintaining the highest goal-reaching rates throughout training among the compared methods -- including in the final stage, where the learning policy operates without any baseline support.

items.title: Causally Evaluating the Learnability of Formal Language Tasks
items.description: Language models, as multi-task learners, acquire a wide range of abilities during training. A fundamental question is how much task-specific data is needed to learn a given task. Answering this for natural language is difficult: tasks are hard to delineate and can confound one another. These serve as a methodologica
items.summary: Language models, as multi-task learners, acquire a wide range of abilities during training. A fundamental question is how much task-specific data is needed to learn a given task. Answering this for natural language is difficult: tasks are hard to delineate and can confound one another. To rigorously investigate the relationship between data frequency and learnability, we turn to a controlled setting using formal languages induced from probabilistic finite automata. These serve as a methodological testbed to demonstrate that standard correlational evaluation practices are inherently flawed. To enable causal analysis, we introduce the binning semiring, an algebraic object that lets us control how often a targeted property occurs in a sampled corpus. We formulate the experimental pipeline as a causal graphical model and derive decomposed Kullback-Leibler divergence metrics to measure the learnability of specific sub-tasks. Our experiments show that evaluating learnability without causal intervention leads to incorrect conclusions due to confounders in correlational analysis, and serve as a warning about correlational pitfalls in natural-language settings.
items.author: Vésteinn Snæbjarnarson, Anej Svete, Josef Valvoda, Reda Boumasmoud, Brian DuSell, Ryan Cotterell
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: evaluation

items.title: Rethinking the Divergence Regularization in LLM RL
items.description: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a key component of post-training large language models (LLMs). In practice, LLM RL is often off-policy because of training-inference mismatch and policy staleness, making trust-region control essential for stable optimization. Mainstream methods such as PPO and GRPO approximate this control with a ratio-clipping mechanism, but the importance ratio can be a poor proxy for distributional shift in long-tailed vocabularies. Recent work such as DPPO addresses th
items.summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a key component of post-training large language models (LLMs). In practice, LLM RL is often off-policy because of training-inference mismatch and policy staleness, making trust-region control essential for stable optimization. Mainstream methods such as PPO and GRPO approximate this control with a ratio-clipping mechanism, but the importance ratio can be a poor proxy for distributional shift in long-tailed vocabularies. Recent work such as DPPO addresses this mismatch by replacing ratio-based clipping with a divergence-based mask, yielding a trust region defined by the sampled token's absolute probability shift. However, DPPO still relies on a hard mask: once a token crosses the trust-region boundary in a harmful direction, its gradient is discarded rather than corrected. To address this, we propose Divergence Regularized Policy Optimization (DRPO), which replaces the hard mask with a smooth advantage-weighted quadratic regularizer on policy shift. DRPO preserves the same trust-region geometry as DPPO while inducing bounded, continuous gradient weights that attenuate diverging updates and provide corrective signals beyond the boundary. Experiments across model scales, architectures, and precision settings show that DRPO improves the stability and efficiency of LLM RL training.
items.author: Jiarui Yao, Xiangxin Zhou, Penghui Qi, Wee Sun Lee, Liefeng Bo, Tianyu Pang
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm

trendEntities.title: PTL-Diffusion: Manifold-Aware Diffusion with Periodic Terminal Laws
normalizedKeys: title:ptl diffusion manifold aware diffusion with periodic terminal laws

items.title: PTL-Diffusion: Manifold-Aware Diffusion with Periodic Terminal Laws
items.description: Standard diffusion models typically use a single time-homogeneous Gaussian terminal distribution as the reference law for generation. While this choice is analytically convenient and empirically powerful, it provides little explicit structure for data concentrated near low-dimensional manifolds, where different regions of the data distribution may correspond to distinct local geometric or semantic factors. As a result, the reverse model must recover manifold-level structure almost entirely from
items.summary: Standard diffusion models typically use a single time-homogeneous Gaussian terminal distribution as the reference law for generation. While this choice is analytically convenient and empirically powerful, it provides little explicit structure for data concentrated near low-dimensional manifolds, where different regions of the data distribution may correspond to distinct local geometric or semantic factors. As a result, the reverse model must recover manifold-level structure almost entirely from an unstructured terminal reference distribution. We propose PTL-Diffusion, a proof-of-concept diffusion framework whose forward noising process converges to a nonconstant periodic family of Gaussian terminal laws rather than to a single invariant law. Unlike a phase-conditioned DDPM, where phase information only enters the denoising network while the forward process remains unchanged, PTL-Diffusion embeds phase structure directly into the forward noising dynamics. The proposed construction remains close to standard denoising diffusion models: for a periodically forced Ornstein--Uhlenbeck-type forward process, we derive closed-form forward marginals, the limiting periodic Gaussian terminal family, and explicit Gaussian reverse posteriors, enabling standard noise-prediction training. We also introduce an invariant-average regularization term coupling the phase-conditioned reverse dynamics through the averaged periodic reference law. Experiments on torus and cylinder point-cloud benchmarks and the Olivetti face dataset show that PTL-Diffusion improves manifold-level distributional matching over matched DDPM baselines, reducing phase-conditioned errors, feature-space covariance errors, and nearest-neighbour manifold distances. These results suggest structured terminal reference laws as a promising direction, while motivating more expressive phase constructions and larger-scale evaluations.
items.author: Danqi Zhuang, Jisui Huang, Xiaoyue Xi, Andrew Kiggins, Xiaojie Wang, Ke Chen, Yue Wu

trendEntities.title: AHA-WAM:Asynchronous Horizon-Adaptive World-Action Modeling with Observation-Guided Context Routing

items.title: AHA-WAM:Asynchronous Horizon-Adaptive World-Action Modeling with Observation-Guided Context Routing
items.description: World-action models have emerged as a promising paradigm for robot manipulation, jointly modeling visual scene dynamics and actions to inject physical priors into policy learning. However, existing world-action models couple world prediction and action execution at the same temporal resolution, forcing the world branch to model near-term frame variations that are redundant and weakly informative. We posit that strictly binding world prediction and action execution to the same temporal rhythm may
items.summary: World-action models have emerged as a promising paradigm for robot manipulation, jointly modeling visual scene dynamics and actions to inject physical priors into policy learning. However, existing world-action models couple world prediction and action execution at the same temporal resolution, forcing the world branch to model near-term frame variations that are redundant and weakly informative. We posit that strictly binding world prediction and action execution to the same temporal rhythm may underutilize the potential of the video branch for embodied control. Therefore, we propose AHA-WAM, an Asynchronous Horizon-Adaptive World-Action Model built on a dual Diffusion Transformer (DiT) architecture that reorganizes world-action modeling around this temporal asymmetry. AHA-WAM instantiates the video DiT as a low-frequency world planner that maintains rolling key-value memory over past observations and exposes reusable layerwise latent context encoding long-horizon scene evolution, while a high-frequency action DiT executes short action chunks in closed loop by querying this context through layerwise joint attention. To support asynchronous execution, we introduce horizon-adaptive offset training and Observation-Guided Video-Context Routing (OVCR), which together let the action expert exploit long-horizon world context while remaining responsive to real-time execution state without rerunning the video DiT. Experiments on RoboTwin and real-world manipulation tasks show that AHA-WAM achieves state-of-the-art performance without any robot-data pretraining, attaining 92.80% average success on RoboTwin and 78.3% success across 4 real-world tasks, while reaching 24.17 Hz closed-loop control with a 4.59x speedup over Fast-WAM.
items.author: Jisong Cai, Long Ling, Shiwei Chu, Zhongshan Liu, Jiayue Kang, Zhixuan Liang, Wenjie Xu, Yinan Mao, Weinan Zhang, Xiaokang Yang, Ru Ying, Ran Zheng, Yao Mu

trendEntities.title: Evaluation Cards: An Interpretive Layer for AI Evaluation Reporting
normalizedKeys: title:evaluation cards an interpretive layer for ai evaluation reporting

items.title: Evaluation Cards: An Interpretive Layer for AI Evaluation Reporting
items.description: AI evaluation results are produced at scale but reported inconsistently across leaderboards, model cards, benchmark papers, and company blogs. The cost is interpretive: readers cannot reliably compare results across sources, identify what a report omits, or trace an aggregate claim to its underlying evidence. Recent efforts address isolated components but leave three gaps: they cover only narrow slices of the evaluation lifecycle and do not compose into a single interpretable record; they specif
items.summary: AI evaluation results are produced at scale but reported inconsistently across leaderboards, model cards, benchmark papers, and company blogs. The cost is interpretive: readers cannot reliably compare results across sources, identify what a report omits, or trace an aggregate claim to its underlying evidence. Recent efforts address isolated components but leave three gaps: they cover only narrow slices of the evaluation lifecycle and do not compose into a single interpretable record; they specify static representations that do not differentiate the questions different stakeholders bring to the same evidence; and they remain proposals on paper, lacking the extraction infrastructure required for adoption at scale. We present \EvalCards{}, an operational reporting layer that composes benchmark metadata, evaluation run data, and model metadata into a unified record. We (1) derive a reporting schema from a structured review of 52 papers and 10 stakeholder interviews, (2) implement four interpretive signals (reproducibility, documentation completeness, provenance and risk, and score comparability), rendered through reader modes calibrated to research and non-research audiences, and (3) deploy a monitoring tool that applies \EvalCards{} across 5,816 models, 635 benchmarks, and 101,843 results, surfacing systematic gaps in current reporting practice.

items.title: Topological Neural Operators
items.description: We introduce Topological Neural Operators (TNOs), a principled framework for operator learning on cell complexes that lifts neural operators (NOs) from functions on points and/or edges to topological domains. TNOs represent data as features defined on cells of varying dimension and model their interactions through Discrete Exterior Calculus, enabling explicit cross-dimensional coupling via gradient-, curl-, and divergence-type operators. The key design principle is to decouple where information
items.summary: We introduce Topological Neural Operators (TNOs), a principled framework for operator learning on cell complexes that lifts neural operators (NOs) from functions on points and/or edges to topological domains. TNOs represent data as features defined on cells of varying dimension and model their interactions through Discrete Exterior Calculus, enabling explicit cross-dimensional coupling via gradient-, curl-, and divergence-type operators. The key design principle is to decouple where information flows, as governed by fixed topological operators, from how it is transformed (which is learned), yielding models that respect the geometric support of physical quantities and expose conservation and compatibility structure. We further propose Hierarchical TNOs (HTNOs), which incorporate learned coarse complexes to propagate long-range and topology-dependent information. Our framework subsumes existing NOs as a special case, providing a unified perspective on operator learning across discretizations. Across a range of PDE benchmarks, including irregular-geometry flow problems, TNOs and HTNOs improve accuracy; controlled studies further isolate the benefits of native higher-rank and topological structure. Project page: https://circle-group.github.io/research/TNO

trendEntities.title: Echo-Memory: A Controlled Study of Memory in Action World Models

items.title: Echo-Memory: A Controlled Study of Memory in Action World Models
items.description: We present \textbf{Echo-Memory}, a controlled study of memory mechanisms in action-conditioned world models. These models generate multi-segment videos from a first frame, text prompt, and camera-action sequence, but their central failure is often memory rather than local image synthesis: after the camera leaves and returns, the scene or salient object may silently change. Existing memory designs are hard to compare because gains are entangled with backbone, training, retrieval, and evaluation d
items.summary: We present \textbf{Echo-Memory}, a controlled study of memory mechanisms in action-conditioned world models. These models generate multi-segment videos from a first frame, text prompt, and camera-action sequence, but their central failure is often memory rather than local image synthesis: after the camera leaves and returns, the scene or salient object may silently change. Existing memory designs are hard to compare because gains are entangled with backbone, training, retrieval, and evaluation differences. Echo-Memory fixes the action-to-video interface and varies only how history is stored and read by the generator. Under a shared video diffusion backbone, optimizer, camera-action representation, sampler, and evaluation pipeline, we compare raw context, compression-based memory, spatial summaries with different read-out paths, and state-space recurrence. This matched matrix separates four otherwise conflated axes: \emph{capacity}, \emph{compression}, \emph{read-out}, and \emph{recurrence}. We also evaluate memory through a three-branch protocol: replay quality, in-domain loop revisit, and open-domain return probes. The branches routinely disagree, showing that replay fidelity is not a sufficient proxy for remembering a world. Three findings follow. Raw context is a strong capacity baseline and improves open-domain return far more than it improves replay metrics. Compactness is not a free substitute for capacity: aggressive spatial and hybrid-compression memories lose the salient evidence needed for return. Finally, block-wise state-space recurrence is the strongest open-domain return mechanism in our matrix, showing that the structure of implicit memory matters as much as the decision to use it. These results provide a compact protocol for studying memory in action world models beyond isolated replay metrics.

normalizedKeys: title:fase fast adaptive semantic entropy for code quality

items.title: FASE: Fast Adaptive Semantic Entropy for Code Quality
items.description: Multi-agent code generation offers a promising paradigm for autonomous software development by simulating the human software engineering lifecycle. However, system reliability remains hindered by LLM hallucinations and error propagation across interacting agents. While semantic entropy provides a principled way to quantify uncertainty without ground-truth answers, current methods often rely on costly LLM-driven equivalence checks. items.summary: Multi-agent code generation offers a promising paradigm for autonomous software development by simulating the human software engineering lifecycle. However, system reliability remains hindered by LLM hallucinations and error propagation across interacting agents. While semantic entropy provides a principled way to quantify uncertainty without ground-truth answers, current methods often rely on costly LLM-driven equivalence checks. In this work, we introduce Fast Adaptive Semantic Entropy (FASE), a novel metric that approximates functional correctness based on the minimum spanning tree of structural and semantic dissimilarity graphs. Evaluations on HumanEval and BigCodeBench demonstrate that FASE outperforms state-of-the-art semantic entropy by LLM entailment, achieving a 25% average improvement in Spearman correlation and a 19% increase in ROCAUC score against Pass@1 from ground-truth test cases when using the Qwen3-Embedding-8B model. Furthermore, by eliminating costly LLM-driven equivalence evaluation, FASE incurs negligible computational overhead, requiring only approximately 0.3% of the runtime cost of traditional semantic entropy approaches. These results position FASE as a practical, cost-effective solution for optimizing uncertainty quantification in real-world multi-agent workflows.
items.author: Shizhe Lin, Ladan Tahvildari

trendEntities.title: Who Earns the Safety? Intervention-Aware Quantum Predictive Control with Safety Attribution
normalizedKeys: title:who earns the safety intervention aware quantum predictive control with safety attribution

items.title: Who Earns the Safety? Intervention-Aware Quantum Predictive Control with Safety Attribution
items.description: Hard safety filters are increasingly placed downstream of learned controllers to guarantee constraint satisfaction at run time. Yet a filtered controller that never violates a constraint may still have learned nothing about safety: the filter can silently repair an incompetent upstream policy, so that post-filter success measures the filter, not the policy. We argue that safe policy learning should ask who earns the safety - the policy or its protective layers - and we make this question measura
items.summary: Hard safety filters are increasingly placed downstream of learned controllers to guarantee constraint satisfaction at run time. Yet a filtered controller that never violates a constraint may still have learned nothing about safety: the filter can silently repair an incompetent upstream policy, so that post-filter success measures the filter, not the policy. We argue that safe policy learning should ask who earns the safety - the policy or its protective layers - and we make this question measurable. We introduce Intervention-Aware Variational Quantum Differentiable Predictive Control (IA-VQC-DPC), which (i) trains a compact variational quantum circuit (VQC) policy under a primal-dual intervention budget that penalizes reliance on a differentiable Control-Barrier-Function (CBF) projection, and (ii) is evaluated with a safety-attribution protocol that decomposes the executed-trajectory correction into a CBF term and a deployment runtime-guard term, and stress-tests the policy with guard-off evaluation. On closed-loop, high-fidelity BOPTEST building-control emulators (5 seeds, 60 episodes per method), intervention-aware training significantly lowers the quantum policy's raw pre-filter violation and total safety-layer reliance (both p < 10^-4) with no significant energy regression; at an equal approximately 400-parameter budget the quantum policy is significantly safer and more comfortable than a matched classical policy. Guard-off evaluation confirms the improvement is policy-level and exposes a valuable negative result: a learned differentiable energy head is only safe when paired with a distribution-aware runtime guard. The attribution protocol is general beyond quantum policies and buildings.
items.author: Yifan Wang

trendEntities.title: SIGA: Self-Evolving Coding-Agent Adapters for Scientific Simulation
normalizedKeys: title:siga self evolving coding agent adapters for scientific simulation

items.title: SIGA: Self-Evolving Coding-Agent Adapters for Scientific Simulation
items.description: Advanced scientific simulators expose specialized input languages that turn simulation goals into executable configurations, but learning them can cost domain scientists hours to days. We study simulator setup as a problem of agent-tool interface grounding: what minimal simulator-specific adaptations are needed for an off-the-shelf coding agent to operate real scientific software? Our intuition is that coding agents already know how to navigate files, edit code, run commands, and repair outputs,
items.summary: Advanced scientific simulators expose specialized input languages that turn simulation goals into executable configurations, but learning them can cost domain scientists hours to days. We study simulator setup as a problem of agent-tool interface grounding: what minimal simulator-specific adaptations are needed for an off-the-shelf coding agent to operate real scientific software? Our intuition is that coding agents already know how to navigate files, edit code, run commands, and repair outputs, but they lack the simulator's executable contract: its vocabulary, structural constraints, validation rules, and termination conditions. We introduce SIGA, a Simulator-Interface Grounding Adapter that supplies this contract through retrieval, procedural memory, in-trajectory validation, and validation-enforced termination. We primarily evaluate SIGA on GEOS, an open-source multiphysics simulator used in subsurface science. SIGA produces a complete GEOS deck in about five minutes with TreeSim above 0.90, matching an extended-budget human expert who took about three hours, a roughly 36x wall-clock speedup. On a harder held-out set, grounding raises TreeSim from 0.720 to 0.789, a roughly 10% relative gain over the bare agent, and can reduce the across-seed standard deviation by 16x. Self-evolution further improves SIGA by rewriting adapter contents from prior trajectories, yielding the highest held-out GEOS mean and matching or outperforming the strongest hand-designed configuration. Transfers to OpenFOAM and LAMMPS show that the dominant mechanism shifts by interface: validation matters most when structural completeness is the bottleneck, while memory and retrieval matter most when domain correctness is the bottleneck. These results suggest that lightweight, self-improvable grounding layers can turn general coding agents into practical operators of scientific software.
items.author: Matthew Ho, Brian Liu, Jixuan Chen, Audrey Wang, Lianhui Qin

trendEntities.title: Discovering Functionally Selective Brain Regions with a Deep Topographic Multimodal Model
normalizedKeys: title:discovering functionally selective brain regions with a deep topographic multimodal model

items.title: Discovering Functionally Selective Brain Regions with a Deep Topographic Multimodal Model
items.description: Nearby neurons in cortex share similar response profiles, producing systematic spatial organization across sensory and cognitive systems. Recent topographic models reproduce aspects of this structure but remain unimodal and spatially constrain each layer separately, yielding fragmented maps that capture neither the contiguity of cortical processing streams nor their integration across modalities. We introduce Topo-Omni, a topographic multimodal model in which visual, auditory, and language/cogni
items.summary: Nearby neurons in cortex share similar response profiles, producing systematic spatial organization across sensory and cognitive systems. Recent topographic models reproduce aspects of this structure but remain unimodal and spatially constrain each layer separately, yielding fragmented maps that capture neither the contiguity of cortical processing streams nor their integration across modalities. We introduce Topo-Omni, a topographic multimodal model in which visual, auditory, and language/cognitive processing share a single contiguous in-silico sheet. Built by fine-tuning a pretrained foundation model with a spatial smoothness objective, this architecture develops clusters across modalities that are consistent with human neuroimaging, from sensory to cognitive systems. Driving or suppressing a cluster selectively biases or impairs perception, paralleling human intervention studies. Finally, we use our model to screen for novel clusters in-silico and discover new natural landscape and animal networks which we validate in human data. A single spatial principle thus organizes representations across modalities and processing stages, yielding testable hypotheses about cortical organization.
items.author: Badr AlKhamissi, Johannes Mehrer, Lara Marinov, Ahmed Abdelaal, Abdulkadir Gokce, Martin Schrimpf
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: rag, fine-tuning
